title: Key concepts: disease and demography
description: Check your knowledge on disease and demography!

assessment_items.question_content:
The plague hit Europe hardest during the

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Spread of disease relied on the existence of many wide-ranging political and economic interactions.
Not all contagious diseases spread rapidly.

choices.content: the rapid spread of a disease through a community

assessment_items.question_content:
Trade helped to spread the plague because

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Plague cannot live outside a human or animal host.

choices.content: trade routes had a steady supply of hosts traveling along them to help spread the plague
plague bacteria took a long time to kill its host, so could travel long distances in an individual person or animal
plague bacteria were able to survive in the goods traders carried without infecting the traders themselves

assessment_items.question_content:
As a result of the plague, Europe

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The plague killed a large portion of Europe’s population

choices.content: saw a dramatic increase in population
experienced better relations between majority and minority groups

assessment_items.question_content:
The reason historians tend to focus studies of the Black Death on Europe is that

 [[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The plague would have caused similar death rates in any populations it infected.

choices.content: the plague didn’t spread anywhere else
there aren’t as many sources documenting it elsewhere
it was substantially worse in Europe
